title: Houses for Rent in West Park, Cleveland
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in West Park, Cleveland OH
metaDescription: Houses for rent in West Park, Cleveland (44111/44126) — rent ranges, RTA access, and what renters should know. Local rental team. Get in touch today.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent West Park cleveland
answerBox: West Park is a residential district on Cleveland's far west side that includes the Jefferson and Kamm's Corners areas. Houses here are mostly early-to-mid-1900s single-family homes, and citywide Cleveland rent for a 2-bedroom runs around $1,100 per Zumper's July 2026 report, with West Park's housing stock generally in that range or below.

sections.h2: What is West Park, and where does it sit in Cleveland?
body: West Park is one of Cleveland's largest west-side residential areas, encompassing the smaller Jefferson and Kamm's Corners planning districts near the city's western border with Fairview Park and North Olmsted. The area developed in the early-to-mid 1900s as streetcar suburbs annexed into the city, which is why the housing stock leans toward brick and frame single-family bungalows and colonials on modest lots.

The district sits close to Cleveland Hopkins International Airport to the south and is bounded by the Rocky River valley (part of Cleveland Metroparks) to the west, giving it a more suburban feel than east-side neighborhoods closer to downtown.

sections.h2: How much does it cost to rent a house in West Park?
body: West Park doesn't have its own published rent index, so the closest grounded benchmark is citywide Cleveland data. Per Zumper's July 4, 2026 report, the Cleveland median asking rent is $1,250/month overall, with 2-bedroom units around $1,100 and 3-bedroom units around $1,350. RentCafe's July 2, 2026 report on professionally-managed apartments puts the citywide average considerably higher, at $1,564/month for 787 square feet, reflecting a different mix of newer buildings. West Park's older single-family housing stock tends to price closer to the Zumper end of that range.

For comparison, our own selection of rental homes across Cleveland runs from about $700 to $1,800 a month, with most homes in the $1,000 range — mostly 2- and 3-bedroom single-family houses, duplexes, triplexes, and fourplexes.

West Park is served by Greater Cleveland RTA bus routes running along Lorain Avenue, Puritas Avenue, and Rocky River Drive into downtown and connecting suburbs, per GCRTA's published route network. It's not on the Red Line, but riders can connect to rail service at nearby West Park or Triskett-area stops depending on the specific route. Interstate 71 and I-480 both run near the district, making it a straightforward drive to downtown, the airport, or western suburbs like North Olmsted and Westlake.

items: **Location:** Far west side, bordering Fairview Park and North Olmsted
**Transit:** GCRTA bus routes along Lorain Ave, Puritas Ave, and Rocky River Dr
**Highways:** Near I-71 and I-480; close to Cleveland Hopkins International Airport
**Green space:** Adjacent to the Rocky River Reservation (Cleveland Metroparks)

sections.h2: What kind of housing is available in West Park?
body: West Park's housing stock is dominated by early-to-mid-1900s single-family houses, many with front porches, small yards, and basements typical of Cleveland's west-side streetcar-era construction. There's a mix of bungalows and colonials, with some duplexes scattered throughout. If you're comparing neighborhoods, [our full guide to houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) breaks down options by bedroom count and budget across the whole city.

sections.h2: Section 8 and voucher-friendly rentals near West Park
body: Every home we work with across Greater Cleveland accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready — that's true whether a home is on the east side or west side of the city. Our current selection is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast neighborhoods, with a smaller number of west-side homes, so availability specifically in West Park varies. Renters interested in vouchers anywhere in Cleveland can read our [Section 8 housing guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) for how CMHA's Housing Choice Voucher program works locally.
If you're looking for a rental in or near West Park, [tell our leasing team what you need](/p/book-showing) and we'll let you know what's currently available across the areas we serve.

faq.q: Is West Park a good area to find a rental house in Cleveland?
faq.a: West Park is one of Cleveland's larger far-west-side residential districts, with housing stock dating mostly to the early-to-mid 1900s. It includes the Jefferson and Kamm's Corners areas and sits near the Rocky River Reservation and Cleveland Hopkins Airport. Renters should compare specific listings for condition, size, and price rather than relying on neighborhood reputation alone.

faq.q: How much is rent for a house in West Park, Cleveland?
faq.a: There's no West Park-specific rent index, but citywide Cleveland data from Zumper (July 2026) shows a 2-bedroom median around $1,100/month and a 3-bedroom around $1,350/month. Actual West Park listings vary by condition and size; check current listings for exact pricing.

faq.q: Does Rent Finder Cleveland works with homes in West Park?
faq.a: Our current selection is concentrated in Cleveland's east and southeast neighborhoods, with a smaller presence on the west side. Availability in West Park specifically varies over time. Tell our team what you're looking for and we'll point you to current options across the areas we serve.

faq.q: Do West Park rentals accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Acceptance depends on the individual landlord — Ohio has no statewide law requiring landlords to accept vouchers, and Cleveland does not currently have a source-of-income protection ordinance. Every home we work with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, regardless of which Cleveland neighborhood it's in.
